bugs.title: Duplicate itinerary items created for helicopter tour
bugs.description: Day 9 has two nearly identical helicopter tour items: 'Private Helicopter Tour: The Vision Flight' and 'Private Luxury Helicopter Tour'. The AI called create_itinerary_items twice in the same response with overlapping content. The deduplication logic caught identical items but these had slightly different titles/descriptions so both were persisted.
bugs.expected: A single helicopter tour item on Day 9
bugs.actual: Two helicopter tour items on Day 9 with slightly different titles
bugs.api_command: SendMessage (planning mode, Dubai dining + helicopter tour message)

bugs.title: Chat history missing 2 of 5 user messages
bugs.description: GetChatHistory returned 8 messages (4 user + 4 assistant) but 5 user messages were sent via SendMessage. The 'private dining in Maldives' (step 3a) and 'Dubai dining + helicopter' (step 3b) messages and their responses are missing from the persisted history. Only selection, seaplane logistics, and both companion messages were preserved.

ux_issues.description: The AI assumed Toronto (YYZ) as departure city without asking. The persona is based in London. While the recommend_booking tool may default to a Canadian city, the AI should have asked or used London as the departure given the persona context.
ux_issues.suggestion: AI should either ask for departure city or infer from persona context (London-based investment banker).

ai_behavior_issues.issue: AI suggested Booking.com links for ultra-luxury Maldives resorts. The persona explicitly notes that suggesting 'check Booking.com' for Soneva Fushi would be tone-deaf, as these properties are best booked through travel advisors or direct.
ai_behavior_issues.context: The recommend_booking tool generated Booking.com affiliate links for Waldorf Astoria Ithaafushi and Atlantis The Royal. While this is the tool's default behavior, the AI did not caveat that direct booking or a luxury travel advisor would yield better rates and villa availability.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: Second expert handoff on message 2 was redundant — same expert (Julian) was already active from message 1
ai_behavior_issues.context: The AI called suggest_expert again with the same region_code (MV) and themes (food, luxury, romance) on the second planning message, resulting in a duplicate personaSwitch event for the same expert already in conversation.

usefulness_evaluation.narrative: The AI demonstrated exceptional luxury calibration throughout. It correctly identified ultra-luxury properties (Waldorf Astoria Ithaafushi Stella Maris villas, Soneva Fushi Water Retreats, Atlantis The Royal Sky Pool Villas), knew property-specific dining experiences (Terra treetop pods, Flying Sauces zipline dining, Cinema Paradiso), understood Maldives logistics (seaplane vs yacht transfer by atoll, daylight-only seaplane ops), and recommended genuinely exclusive Dubai dining (Tresind Studio, Moonrise 12-seat counter, Orfali Bros). Companion mode responses were appropriately concise and anniversary-aware. The itinerary had luxury pacing with breathing room between activities. Minor deductions for duplicate itinerary items and the redundant expert handoff.
